newsSince: No new thesis-changing datapoint since June 9; the record 1Q26 print (server/memory + Apple, PCB order overflow, AI infrastructure demand) was already reported May 11-12 and captured in the prior June-9 up-move. May monthly revenue continues the record trajectory but adds confirmation, not new alpha.

sources.label: Apple/AI server demand power Tripod record 1Q26

verdicts.rankRationale: On the residual-alpha lens Tripod still screens as fundamentals-outrunning-recognition: server/networking PCB order-overflow and accelerating revenue confirm the tight-supply central-node thesis at a moderate +70% YTD. No genuinely new datapoint since the June-9 up-move, so hold with a small + as the thesis stays intact.
verdicts.bull: Server/AI + Apple PCB order overflow; record 1Q26; tight-supply central node, still under-followed.
verdicts.neutral: Record print already known; growth confirmation rather than new evidence.
verdicts.bear: Cyclical PCB exposure; momentum could fade if AI server build digests.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI server/networking PCB tight supply and capacity expansion

verdicts.rankRationale: On the master bottleneck list Tripod sits at a high rank #3; the record 1Q26 confirmed the HDI/boards execution leg this page told us to watch, but it is confirmation, not thesis-changing, and was already priced into the June-9 hold. Moderate +70% YTD keeps risk/reward balanced; hold.
verdicts.bull: Top-tier HDI/PCB scarcity beneficiary; record results; relatively measured YTD vs substrate peers.
verdicts.neutral: Already high rank; execution confirmed but no new bottleneck evidence.
verdicts.bear: Rank #3 leaves little upside if AI server PCB demand or pricing softens.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Sustained AI server/HDI PCB demand and pricing
